data.title: Expedia pops 11% as it shakes off the online travel sector slump
data.description:
Expedia (NASDAQ:EXPE) rallied in early trading on Friday after the online travel company topped consensus estimates with its earnings report. Total gross bookings were up 6% to $28.8 billion and room nights growth was up 10% during the quarter. Adjusted EBITDA came in 5% higher than a year ago at $786 million.

During the earnings conference call (transcript), Expedia executives were cautious about travel demand, consistent with the statements seen by others in the travel industry. "We have seen a more challenging macro environment and a slowdown in travel demand," warned. CEO Ariane Gorin. Expedia (EXPE) also said that it has seen some headwinds from new pricing display regulations that kicked off on July 1st in California.

Looking ahead, Expedia (EXPE) sees Q3 gross bookings and revenue growth in the 3% to 5% range. The company expects approximately 100 basis points of deleverage to Q3 EBITDA and EBIT margins. As for the key metric, Expedia (EXPE) guided for full-year gross bookings to be at the low end of previous guidance range of mid-to-high single digits.

Shares of Expedia (EXPE) were up 10.75% in the premarket session after showing some weakness in the six weeks leading into the earnings report. The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating on Expedia (EXPE) has been flashing Buy.

data.title: goeasy Non-GAAP EPS of C$4.10, revenue of C$378M; issues FY24 and beyond outlook
data.description:
• goeasy press release (OTCPK:EHMEF): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of C$4.10.
  • Revenue of C$378M (+24.8% Y/Y).
  • Loan Originations of C$827 million, up 24% from C$667 million.
  • Loan Growth of C$286 million, up 37% from C$210 million.
  • Loan Portfolio of C$4.14 billion, up 29% from C$3.20 billion.
  • The efficiency ratio for the second quarter of 2024 was a record 26.9%, an improvement of 430 bps from 31.2% in the second quarter of 2023, reflecting an increase in operating leverage.
  • Return on equity during the quarter was 23.3%, compared to 24.0% in the second quarter of 2023. Adjusted return on equity1 was 25.4% in the quarter, an increase of 120 bps from 24.2% in the same period of 2023.

  • The forecast outlined below is based on the Company's expected domestic organic growth plan and does not include the impact of any future mergers or acquisitions, or the associated gains or losses related to its investments.

  • Expressed in Canadian dollars

data.title: AirSculpt Technologies CEO Todd Magazine steps down
data.description:
• AirSculpt Technologies (NASDAQ:AIRS) said its CEO, Todd Magazine, has stepped down from his role.
  • The firm said its CFO, Dennis Dean, will helm the company on an interim basis, until a permanent successor is named.

data.title: Minerva S.A. reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Minerva S.A. press release (OTCPK:MRVSY): Q2 consolidated gross revenue in the second quarter of 2024 was R$ 8.2 billion
  • Net revenue was R$ 7.7 billion in the period, up by 6.7% on the previous quarter, and 5.4% up year on year.
  • EBITDA was R$ 744.6 million, with an EBITDA margin of 9.7%, up by 18.4% on the previous quarter.

data.title: Cineplex GAAP EPS of -C$0.33, revenue of C$277.34M
data.description:
• Cineplex press release (OTCPK:CPXGF): Q2 GAAP EPS of -C$0.33.

  • Delivered BPP of C$13.11 and CPP of C$9.56, both all-time quarterly records.
